004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonl;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.math.*;
021import java.nio.charset.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.commons.io.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
026
027/**
028 * Reference implementation of the public {@link TokenWriter} surface for the JSONL / NDJSON
029 * format.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Wraps a {@link JsonTokenWriter} and appends a newline after every top-level value emit.  The
033 * "top-level value" boundary is detected by tracking when an emit returns the cursor to
034 * {@code depth == 0}.
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
038 * </ul>
039 */
040@SuppressWarnings({
041   "resource" // The writer's underlying Writer/OutputStream is owned by the caller via try-with-resources on the writer itself; Eclipse JDT flags the inner stream as unclosed but that's by design.
042})
043public class JsonlTokenWriter implements TokenWriter {
044
045   private final JsonTokenWriter delegate;
046   private final Writer out;
047   private final Closeable owned;
048   private int depth;
049   private boolean closed;
050
051   /**
052    * Constructor.
053    *
054    * @param out The writer to emit JSONL to.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
055    * @param settings The output-formatting settings.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
056    */
057   public JsonlTokenWriter(Writer out, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings) {
058      this(out, settings, null);
059   }
060
061   /**
062    * Internal constructor that records an underlying {@link Closeable} the writer should close on
063    * shutdown (e.g. a file stream opened by {@link #forOutput(Object, JsonTokenWriter.Settings)}).
064    *
065    * @param out The writer to emit JSONL to.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
066    * @param settings The output-formatting settings.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
067    * @param owned A resource owned by this writer that should be closed on close, or <jk>null</jk>.
068    */
069   JsonlTokenWriter(Writer out, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings, Closeable owned) {
070      this.delegate = new JsonTokenWriter(out, settings);
071      this.out = out;
072      this.owned = owned;
073   }
074
075   /**
076    * Internal factory that mirrors {@link JsonTokenWriter#forOutput(Object, JsonTokenWriter.Settings)}
077    * so the per-format {@code tokenWriter(...)} factory can hand in any of the supported output
078    * types ({@link Writer}, {@link OutputStream}, {@link File}, {@link StringBuilder}).
079    *
080    * @param output The output object.
081    * @param settings The output-formatting settings.
082    * @return A new {@link JsonlTokenWriter} writing to the resolved underlying writer.
083    * @throws IOException If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
084    */
085   public static JsonlTokenWriter forOutput(Object output, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings) throws IOException {
086      if (output == null)
087         throw new IOException("Output cannot be null.");
088      if (output instanceof Writer w)
089         return new JsonlTokenWriter(w, settings);
090      if (output instanceof OutputStream os)
091         return new JsonlTokenWriter(new OutputStreamWriter(os, StandardCharsets.UTF_8), settings);
092      if (output instanceof File f) {
093         var os = new BufferedOutputStream(new FileOutputStream(f));
094         return new JsonlTokenWriter(new OutputStreamWriter(os, StandardCharsets.UTF_8), settings, os);
095      }
096      if (output instanceof StringBuilder sb)
097         return new JsonlTokenWriter(new StringBuilderWriter(sb), settings);
098      throw new IOException("Cannot convert object of type " + output.getClass().getName() + " to a Writer.");
099   }
100
101   @Override public TokenWriter startObject() throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.startObject(); depth++; return this; }
102   @Override public TokenWriter endObject() throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.endObject(); return endTopLevel(); }
103   @Override public TokenWriter startArray() throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.startArray(); depth++; return this; }
104   @Override public TokenWriter endArray() throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.endArray(); return endTopLevel(); }
105   @Override public TokenWriter fieldName(String name) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.fieldName(name); return this; }
106   @Override public TokenWriter string(String value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.string(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
107   @Override public TokenWriter number(Number value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.number(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
108   @Override public TokenWriter number(long value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.number(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
109   @Override public TokenWriter number(double value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.number(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
110   @Override public TokenWriter number(BigDecimal value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.number(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
111   @Override public TokenWriter number(BigInteger value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.number(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
112   @Override public TokenWriter bool(boolean value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.bool(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
113   @Override public TokenWriter nil() throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.nil(); return scalarTopLevel(); }
114   @Override public TokenWriter binary(byte[] value) throws IOException { assertOpen(); delegate.binary(value); return scalarTopLevel(); }
115
116   @Override
117   public TokenWriter object(Object value) throws IOException {
118      assertOpen();
119      // Walk through THIS writer's structural methods so the depth tracking + trailing-newline
120      // logic fires correctly.  PojoWalker honors the WalkOptions configured on the underlying
121      // delegate's Settings.
122      PojoWalker.walk(this, value, delegate.settingsAccess().walk());
123      return this;
124   }
125
126   @Override public boolean isStreaming() { return true; }
127   @Override public void flush() throws IOException { out.flush(); }
128
129   @Override
130   public void close() throws IOException {
131      if (closed)
132         return;
133      closed = true;
134      try {
135         delegate.close();
136      } finally {
137         if (owned != null)
138            owned.close();
139      }
140   }
141
142   /**
143    * Throws an {@link IOException} if this writer has been {@link #close() closed}.
144    *
145    * @throws IOException If this writer is closed.
146    */
147   private void assertOpen() throws IOException {
148      if (closed)
149         throw new IOException("Token writer is closed.");
150   }
151
152   private TokenWriter endTopLevel() throws IOException {
153      depth--;
154      if (depth == 0)
155         out.write('\n');
156      return this;
157   }
158
159   private TokenWriter scalarTopLevel() throws IOException {
160      if (depth == 0)
161         out.write('\n');
162      return this;
163   }
164}
